category_title: Emotionally disturbed first-grader with trauma-induced behavioral challenges
minimal_context: Jamal is asked to practice the basic alphabet while sitting in a classroom with 25 other students.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Jamal, it is time to do the basic alphabet.

STUDENT: (Shows wobbly pouting lips) I don't want to do the letters.

TUTOR: We need to finish this assignment.

STUDENT: (Looks around at the other students, loudly seeking attention) No!

TUTOR: Please just try to write the first few letters.

STUDENT: (Crying) I can’t do it!

options.A: Provide sequenced instruction in the form of rhymes to help Jamal form the basic alphabet letters during the classroom activity.
options.B: Utilize support personnel, such as a teacher’s assistant or paraprofessional, to assist in the classroom during the basic alphabet activity.
options.C: Implement a color-coded behavior chart to track Jamal’s behavior across seven levels, proceeding from the lowest level of 'Needing Parent Contact' to the highest level of 'Super Principled'.
options.D: Provide large fonts with supporting pictures, reduce the number of alphabet letters to practice, offer access to word banks, and incorporate speech-generating devices.
